candidates.stmt:
CANDIDATE STATEMENT

I am a lifelong Havering resident and I care deeply about the future of our borough. Living here all my life means I understand both the strengths of our community and the challenges we face. I see the pride many residents have in Havering, but I also recognise where improvements and investment are needed. Rainham and Beam Park have a strong community spirit. I have seen this firsthand through events such as the Rainham Remembrance Day Parade and the Christmas celebrations, which I have helped organise, where local people come together to support and celebrate our community. As a mother of two and a grandmother of six, I see Havering from both younger and older generations’ perspectives. I understand the concerns families face today, as well as the importance of protecting opportunities for the next generation. During my time working in accountancy, I saw individuals and businesses face challenges but also grow and succeed when given the right support. Through volunteering with the local foodbank, helping a homeless charity, and taking part in community litter picking, I have met many residents who simply want to be heard and supported. These experiences have strengthened my belief that local decisions must always put residents first. That is why I am standing as a candidate for the Havering Residents Association to be the Residents’ Voice for Beam Park, representing local people rather than national party politics. “Local people deserve a local voice and that is why I am standing.”

I moved to Havering as a 9-year-old from North Woolwich. I grew up in Collier Row and have lived in Rainham for more than 25 years.
I am a scuba diving instructor who is in their element being under the ocean. I am a proud dad and grandad. Spending time with my grandsons reminds me how important it is to protect the values that make a great community...Respect, Pride, Manners, listening and looking out for each other.
I am not afraid of rolling up my sleeves and helping where I can.
Over the years I have Volunteered for foodbanks, homeless shelter and community litter picking also fundraising for Easter eggs and christmas gifts for children in need. I was one of the organisers of the Rainham Village Remembrance Day Parade, also the much Loved Rainham Community Christmas Lights Switch on.
I Am Not A Career Politician
Like many residents, see the impact that lack of government funding has had on our council and local services.
Too often national parties create division when what we really need is people working together to improve where we all live. Vote for a H.R.A resident who can see the issues, not a national party who creates them

Lois is a disability rights advocate, conservationist and artist. Lois has a keen interest on improving the accessibility of council services and improving SEND provision across the borough. Lois is a RSPB member and wants to secure the future of Rainham Marshes against funding challenges and Labour’s new laws which permit developers to destroy nature for cash.

Tito is the Director of Nourishment, a community and creative agency that bridges the gap between creatives and young professionals with cultural institutions through events and content. Tito takes a particular interest in how technology & innovation policy can be leveraged to tackle social challenges, particularly in diverse and low-income communities, and has pursued this through research in academia.

Sarah is a lifelong Upminster resident who studied locally before completing her further education in London, Manchester and Milan. After a period living in Italy, she returned home and has spent the past 20 years working in NHS primary care, including roles in commissioning health services for Havering patients and patient engagement.

Outside of her professional commitments, Sarah is a busy single parent to two young daughters and a carer for her mother, who lives with Alzheimer’s. These personal experiences underpin her strong commitment to improving support for families and carers.

Sarah is a passionate advocate for social justice, environmental responsibility, and the equality and empowerment of girls and women.

In her spare time, Sarah is a later-life learner of the piano and achieved Grade 1 last year—despite, as she puts it, having no discernible musical talent—demonstrating her belief that it is never too late to take on a new challenge. She is also a lover of languages and a proud polyglot, currently working on expanding her repertoire to include Latin and Arabic.

A lifelong Upminster resident, I belong to many environmental organisations, am a member of Upminster Methodist Church and a West Ham season ticket holder. I run a company manufacturing adhesives in Purfleet and am a part-time author, having written six books, mainly about walking and travel.

I am standing for the Green Party because I believe that protecting our planet, its people and wildlife is the most important issue that mankind faces, and because I believe that we need a society and economy that are fair to everyone.

I am a firm believer in all forms of equality, improving public transport, supporting local shops and small businesses, support for science & technology, dealing effectively with the increasing problems of homelessness and preventing cruelty to animals.

Locally I would push for improved bus services, a more effective and less damaging alternative to Thames Crossing Option C, reduced business rates to help local shops, protection of our precious Green Belt and for more social housing.

I believe that no vote is wasted and that every vote the Green Party receives is important as a demonstration of concern for our environment and for social justice

Kim Arrowsmith is a Deputy Headteacher, who has worked in Barking and Dagenham for 7 years. He is very concerned about the chronic underfunding of our public services by the current Tory Government, and particularly the impact this is having on the ability of our schools and hospitals to deliver for the local community in Dagenham and Rainham. He also believes in protecting our vital green spaces from the threat of over development and repurposing abandoned and disused residential and commercial properties as sustainable housing.

Sami is an award-winning AI and technology strategist with extensive experience in the corporate sector. A resident of Elm Park for five years, he wants to use his expertise to help modernise council services, to deliver both value and transparency for all residents.

My Official Statement – Elm Park Candidate

Sidra Bhatti is a dedicated community advocate, social leader, and active member of the UK Labour Party, committed to empowering individuals and strengthening local communities. She firmly believes in fair representation and equal opportunity, working to ensure every resident’s voice is heard and valued.

As the Founder and Director of Rahe Khidmat Organisation, Sidra supports vulnerable families through essential aid, education, and community care initiatives—helping those facing hardship with dignity and compassion. She is also actively involved with various community and charitable organisations, building strong connections and delivering practical support where it is needed most.

Known for her approachable nature and strong communication skills, Sidra regularly engages with residents—offering guidance, connecting individuals to the right services, and promoting new talent, especially among young people. Through her media presence and public speaking, she raises awareness of key social issues and encourages active community participation.

With real-life experience balancing family, professional responsibilities, and community service, Sidra understands the everyday challenges faced by local residents. Her work is guided by honesty, integrity, and a genuine commitment to making a positive difference.

She remains dedicated to building a more inclusive, supportive, and forward-looking Elm Park—where everyone has the opportunity to grow, contribute, and succeed.

Your support means everything—thank you.

Gheorghe is a long time environmental and citizens rights campaigner, heavily involved in the3million campaign to ensure Europeans that have moved to the UK can continue to call this place home without family separation or risk of deportation.

Ghazala moved to UK as a child almost 50 years ago. Having lived in Havering for over 35 years and brought up a family of 4 kids and as a working Mum she knows what pressures ordinary folks face on daily basis. With her kids all now grown up, she is keen to be actively engaged in the local community. She has been a parent school governor and she has supported her husband's work in the NHS. Ghazala left school at 16 to care for her mother and younger siblings and went back into education later in life. She is passionate about everyone deserving a second chance and is particularly keen for the local council to support young people in our community, to unlock their potential. Has had various careers and is currently a dedicated driving instructor.

As someone who has spent most of their life in Havering and Redbridge, I have had the honour of representing constituents across both areas and working with our local communities and other organisations over the past 30 years.

Being A Councillor in Both Havering and Redbridge

I was a councillor in Havering for many years but moved to Redbridge when I started a family. All three of my children were raised and educated in Redbridge, and I served as a councillor in Redbridge for 15 years until I stood down in advance of the 2018 local elections. My time living, and helping constituents, across both boroughs, has allowed me to understand, and work to deal with, the issues we all have.

My Time on The London Assembly

My work in City Hall itself, involves holding the Mayor of London to account, and serving on committees such as the Policing & Crime, and Transport Committees. The most rewarding parts of my role on the London Assembly have included:

*   Working with local and London-wide youth groups, charities, sporting and other organisations, to tackle antisocial behaviour and knife crime. *   Working with local and London-wide homelessness charities, to find practical solutions for homelessness across our area and London. *   Working with local and regional employers, to provide job and training opportunities for residents and especially our young people.

I use my position by giving my time, publicity, making connections, securing assets and funding. If you are involved with or know a group that may benefit from our work, please do contact me and I’m always happy to see what can be done.

Let’s Make Further Progress, Together

It has been a real privilege for me to be in a position where I can make a difference to my local community and the City I love, but I have only been able to make that difference because of your support.

Your local councillor since 2018 and Cabinet Member for Housing and Property. A Harold Hill resident since 1985, I'm committed to putting our community first.

Over 30 years as a local school governor

Board member at BETRA, supporting estate improvements

Delivered upgrades to hundreds of local homes (kitchens, roofs, windows)

Supported new homes for local residents and key projects like the Family Welcome Centre

Led the regeneration project of Hilldene shopping centre.

Protected our green spaces

Stopped development on Tiverton Road Green and Petersfield Avenue

Secured village green status for Gooshays Green, Hitchin Close & Priory Green.

Active with the Harold Hill Wombles, helping keep our area clean and green

Working for Harold Hill residents. I live in Harold Hill and want the best for our area and community.

I have been a very proud resident of Harold Hill my whole life, where I live with my four children.

I actively contribute to the community by running local groups that support those who need it most, providing free items such as mobility aids and food. Helping local people during difficult times means a great deal to me, which is why I'm so excited to be part of the HRA.

My career has spanned many years in recruitment, where I've helped people realise their potential, improve their employability, and pursue their goals. Whether it's connecting someone with their dream role, offering job search advice, rewriting a CV, giving interview tips, or simply being a supportive and positive presence, I always go above and beyond to help others.

Outside of work, I am a mum of four and enjoy spending time with my children, making the most of our local area and creating happy memories together.

I am passionate about Harold Hill and committed to being a strong voice for local people, especially those who feel they haven't been heard.

I will always do my best and put our community first.

I've proudly called Harold Hill home for over 22 years. From the moment I moved here from North London, I felt the strong sense of community and care that makes this area so special to me. It's where my children were born and raised, and where my family runs our local business. I've always felt supported here, and truly value the friendly atmosphere, our beautiful parks, and the deer that make Harold Hill unique.

I am a Civil Engineer working in highways, specialising in infrastructure and development. With over 14 years of experience, I understand how decisions are made, how funding is used, and how we can deliver real, practical improvements to our roads, services, and neighbourhoods. Alongside my professional work, I have always been actively involved in supporting people in the community-helping with forms, job applications, and accessing services, particularly for elderly and low- income residents. Being bilingual has allowed me to support people who might otherwise struggle to be heard.

My personal experiences have shaped who I am. Supporting a brother with learning difficulties and another with mental health challenges has given me resilience, empathy, and a strong sense of responsibility. As a parent of twins, balancing home life, career, and a family business.

I care strongly about protecting and supporting families and strengthening our community. I believe that when people are given fair opportunities and the right support, they can build better lives-not just for themselves, but for others around them.

As a young person who has lived in Havering my whole life, I know what it feels like to be told my voice doesn’t matter which is exactly why I’m standing as a candidate. Too often, young people are left out of decisions that shape their future. I have hope in a community where everyone regardless of age, has a say in what happens next. My main focus is simple, listen to local residents, act on local concerns and work towards a greener, fairer community for people. I believe that Gooshays deserve representatives who understand today’s realities such as the pressure on young people, the need for affordable living and the importance of protecting our environment for future generations. I’m proud to stand for positive change in our local area and I’m ready to work hard for every resident.

Cenred is committed to improving Council services for local residents. He wants Havering to become a more welcoming Borough where people take pride in their environment, building strong community bonds built on tolerance and respect.

candidates.p: Harold Wood Hill Park Residents Association
Harold Wood Hill Park Residents Association
Harold Wood Hill Park Residents Association

candidates.stmt:
I live in Noak Hill, Romford and have three daughters and three grandchildren. After 30 years working in the city, I retired and went to study at Writtle Agricultural College. Armed with my new found qualifications, I started a landscaping company from which I am now retired. In 2010, totally disillusioned with the two-party politics, I stood against Andrew Rosindell as an Independent candidate. In 2014, believing the UK has a better future out of the EU, I stood in the local elections on behalf of UKIP. My success resulted in me representing the ward of Heaton (Harold Hill). In 2016, totally disillusioned with the management of the council and total lack of care towards my ward, I resigned my seat in protest. In 2015, I represented UKIP in the General Election. This time in the constituency of Ilford North.

I recently retired as a director of Romford Golf Club to dedicate myself to the upcoming campaign. I have been closely associated with Romford Football Club for many years and was a main sponsor for several seasons.

My passion has always been canoeing and along with a friend, we started Stubbers Canoe Club some 50 years ago. We still train and compete together but now for Chelmsford Canoe Club. I am qualified for the Olympic white-water course at Lea Valley and still visit occasionally. Recently, I joined Stone Sailing club near Maldon and have enjoyed returning to another sport of my youth.

This is an exciting time to change the path we (UK) have been following for the past 40 or 50 years. The policies on which Reform have built their Contract are based on common sense and sound financial planning...but more of that later

As payroll officer in another local borough I have experience with the strains on council budgets and how much isn't open to negotiation.
As a new father I am particularly interested in improving transport to schools. This ward contains the two highest rated primary schools in the borough and yet the public transport options to them are very limited. I would like to provide alternatives to driving, clearing up the local traffic issues and encouraging healthier options for the children.

I am a retired BT Engineer and I have lived in Havering for 47 years.  I was born in Hackney but we moved to Dagenham where I attended both Primary & Secondary school.

I have been a School Governor at a wide variety of schools for over 30 years and I am currently Vice-Chair at Corbets Tey Special School in Upminster.
I served eight years as a Havering Councillor from 1998 to 2006 where I gained great knowledge of how Council’s work.

I am the Chair of the Ingrebourne Valley  and Hornchurch Country Park Friends Group and a member of Havering Cyclists (London Cycling Campaign) with my preferred mode of transport being the bicycle.

If elected to serve as a Councillor again, my concentration would be on listening to residents’ concerns and being available at regular surgeries held within the ward at least fortnightly.

If not in Administration then I would hope to:

•	Influence Havering Council to create meaningful Overview and Scrutiny Committees with a view to challenging the Administration which currently does not exist.
•	Encourage more Community Groups to play a part in establishing facilities that benefit the Environment in respect to the Green Forum and continue to improve the Active Travel Strategy which has had very little input from the public thus far.
•	Continue to fight for more appropriate funding for young people and adults who currently have little support from an inadequate budget.

candidates.p: Harold Wood Hill Park Residents Association
Harold Wood Hill Park Residents Association
Harold Wood Hill Park Residents Association

candidates.stmt:
What I would like to do for my constituents:

1. No ULEZ (car drivers pay enough taxes)

2. Reduce council tax bills for residents and businesses

3. More money for doctors and nurses on the NHS to keep them here in the UK

4. More NHS dentists

5. Better sentences to fit the crime

6. No university fees for UK citizens

7. Deal with illegal immigration

8. Police need to fight real crime

We are a rich country and our citizens should come first.  Don't waste your vote on the other two parties that have not helped the working class for a number of years now.  

I am retired, aged 67.  I moved to Harold Hill at the age of 10 from North London.  I represented England in Boxing at 18 and have volunteered for the last 10 years now as a coach and competition secretary for a local boxing club to help keep kids off the street and give them good values.  It has been very rewarding to see over the years some of the kids go to university, get good jobs and respect others, which I am proud to see.

I now have time to make a difference to this community.  I think supporting clubs for young and old is good for the community. 

UK citizens should come first! The homeless, veterans, mental health issues, kids and pensioners.

Tories and Labour have let the working class people down.  

The House of Lords need no money from the tax payer, they have not been elected.  Most of them have pensions and other jobs so if they want to be in the House of Lords then they should be doing it voluntarily if they really truly want to help people.  MPs also have to have some of the perks removed, we are all in this together.

Stop wasting UK tax payers money.

VOTE REFORM UK

Marion is a therapist working to support people’s mental health, particularly in the areas of bereavement, depression & anxiety, trauma and with LGBTQIA+ people.

She is a passionate member of Havering Friends of the Earth, and has been a part of campaigning against the East Havering Data Centre proposal and to protect Havering’s green belt.

Marion actively works to promote anti-racism, and is concerned about issues within the borough such as poverty, housing, and protecting the local environment and air quality.

Anish grew up in Aldborough Hatch and has lived in Harold Wood for 5 years. Graduating from King's College London in 2010, he is a dentist in east London and Essex, and worked for many years as an NHS dentist in Harold Hill.

He believes that the decimation of local services, as well as local disruption and environmental destruction caused by huge private interests, and the breakdown of affordable healthcare and housing, are all symptomatic of wider economic collapse.

The Green Party is the only party that supports taxing wealth, not work.

Anish believes that without stopping the rapid redistribution of wealth up to the already extremely wealthy, not only will climate change continue to accelerate and the cost of living continue to rise, but generations to come will never afford decent housing, basic healthcare or dignity in old age.

As a father of two, Alex believes that Harold Wood deserves infrastructure and public services that actually serve its residents, not private interests. He is deeply concerned by the ongoing erosion of our community resources, highlighted by the recent loss of the local public library. In his career as an enterprise architect, Alex's job is to look at large, failing, or inefficient systems, figure out why they are broken, and rebuild them so they work for the people using them. He is bringing this exact approach to the Green Party to fight against privatization, fix failing local services, and protect our local environment.

Carole is a former councillor for Harold Hill, then serving on the Environmental Overview and Scrutiny sub-committee around the first Havering Climate Change Action Plan. Carole is passionate about improving the standard and supply of housing, and quality of life for Heaton residents.

Aziz holds a Masters of Law in legal practice and works in advertising. He has lived in Havering for the majority of his life, witnessing the loss of social responsibility and the difficulty to make ends meet for many famillies on the Hill. Aziz wants to represent our underserved communities.

Daljit lives with his family locally and grew up in rural, outer London. With 20+ years in physical and cyber security, Daljit solves problems and keep people safe. He also volunteers in education, helping children develop STEM skills and build confidence with technology.

Erin is a systems architect working in the energy compliance and servicing sector for over 20 years. A keen environmentalist who is passionate about enabling the community away from a dependency on fossil fuels. Ze also works within the non-profit sector for a queer health, support and social CIC. A true believer in helping to amplify the voices of those wanting to bring change.

I’m standing as a Labour candidate because my entire working life has been rooted in service - supporting young people, disabled groups, older residents and vulnerable families across the public, corporate and charity sectors.

I spent eight years working in Governance in a FTSE 100 company, having moved into that field after a career in buying departments. That experience gave me a deep understanding of how large organisations set their policies, values and mission, and how those principles shape decisions about budgets, safeguarding, procurement and accountability. It taught me how to scrutinise proposals, challenge systems constructively and ensure that decisions remain anchored in fairness and evidence.

When I left the corporate sector, I first transitioned into a sailing charity in South London that worked with under‑privileged young people and disability groups. The aim was to give young people safe, structured opportunities that kept them off the streets and opened doors they would never otherwise have had. It was incredibly rewarding to see some of those young people go on to build long‑term careers in the maritime industry — opportunities that simply wouldn’t have existed without the charity’s support. We also worked closely with the local police on safeguarding and early‑intervention work, including how to spot signs of radicalisation and vulnerability.

I then joined Age UK at national level, initially in a department delivering services for older people across the country and supporting local Age UK partners. This gave me a clear understanding of the challenges older people face and the importance of consistent, high‑quality local provision. I later moved into the Finance and Corporate Team, where I gained a deep understanding of how a major charity manages its resources, governance, risk and organisational priorities. Before redundancy, I moved into the newly formed CIC — the revenue‑raising arm of Age UK that helps fund the charity’s wider good works.

Erin is an IT consultant and mother who has been a homeowner and resident in Havering for over a decade, where her experiences have shaped a strong commitment to social justice and affordable living. She is deeply engaged in thinking about how society functions, and often doesn’t, particularly when it comes to climate change, inequality, and widening wealth gaps that impact future generations. Passionate and outspoken, Erin cares about the imbalance between those who have so much and communities that struggle, and she values meaningful conversations about how real change can happen. Erin believes that hope lies in people, in collective action and the everyday choices that can build a fairer future. She believes that hope IS here, and it is all of us.

Sami is an avid student studying a partly international sixth form curriculum who has lived and studied abroad for five years. As such, he has a deep passion and understanding for cultural representation, and being a young adult, sympathises with the frustration young people have with feeling heard, and their worries about financial uncertainty in the future. He believes that in order to build a future where opportunities are more fair and everyone has equal ability to live contently, it must start with the emerging generations, to create a strong and educated foundation for hope to flourish.

Alex is an experienced social housing professional, having worked for a diverse range of local authorities for over 25 years. He has a particular interest in supporting council tenants to exercise their right to manage, a statutory right which is often not promoted enough by councils throughout England.

Alex is a single parent carer who believes the Council should be doing everything it can to support vulnerable adults and children, including their carers who often suffer in silence.

He is also a longstanding trade union activist with Unison, campaigning for better individual and collective rights in the workplace as well as representing Unison members in workplace disputes.

Alex strongly believes the needs of people can best be met by people whose own needs have been met, therefore it is essential that council services are properly funded.
Manon has experience in change management and process improvement. She has lived in Romford for 9 years and wants Havering to be a place where everyone is welcome, supported and proud of their area.

Daniel has lived in Romford since he moved here as an infant in 1976. He has been active in politics for over 30 years, mostly with the Labour Party which he left last year over their policies on Gaza and immigration. He joined the Green Party as he agrees with their policies on a universal basic income and fighting climate change. He works in Adults Care & Support Commissioning for a local authority and is a long standing trade unionist.

Statement

I have lived in Rainham village for six years and am passionate about improving the local area and supporting the community. I work as a Tutor, Assessor and Internal Quality Assurer and have a keen interest in education policy. It is important to me that young people across Rainham & Wennington have a fair start in life.

Since moving to Rainham in 2016 I have become involved with local community and faith based groups, and support food banks in the area. I also have plans to start a food bank in the coming years. As a Labour campaigner I am always working to improve the quality of life for local people and as a resident I feel Rainham & Wennington is often forgotten by Havering Council. I want our community to have a seat at the table; we have been poorly represented for too long. Over the years residents in Rainham and Wennington have faced many challenges such as diminishing GP and health services, severe flooding, loss of green space, HGVs blighting both villages, threats of overdevelopment, and road safety issues. Through all of these challenges the silence from existing Independent councillors has been deafening. Without proper representation the Tories in the Town Hall have been able to neglect our area unopposed. I’m looking forward to welcoming our neighbours and making sure that as a community we get the service we deserve from the council, from housing associations, and landlords. This area, like the rest of the country, needs us to speak with one voice to improve the lives of ordinary people. That’s why I’m proud to stand as a candidate for the Labour Party.

I have spent my whole life living in Havering, growing up, building my career, and raising my three children alongside our dog and cat. This community is part of who I am.

Over the years, I’ve seen Rainham and Wennington and the wider borough of Havering change, some changes have been positive, others more challenging. Like many people, I’ve thought about moving away, but I never quite can. This is home, and I care deeply about its future.

We have so much on our doorstep, strong community spirit, great local amenities, and excellent connections when we want to travel. But more than that, I believe we can make this an even better place to live, a borough where people feel proud, where families feel safe, and where our children can grow up with real opportunities.

That’s why I’m standing as a local councillor for Havering with the Green Party. I want to help our area thrive, to bring people together, and to make Rainham and Wennington a place we all truly enjoy being part of.

Let’s work together to build a stronger, safer, and more vibrant community.

Kelly has years of experience in youth and community work, community safety, and grassroots projects. She believes in listening first and working with residents to co-design solutions that keep communities safe, supported, and thriving.

I am excited to stand in the 7th May local elections. I’m determined to make Rush Green and Crowlands a great place to grow up and grow old in. Our area has so much potential, but as a father with a young family I share residents’ determination that our streets should feel safer and cleaner.

I have already been fighting hard as part of the Labour Action Team for Rush Green and Crowlands - reporting fly-tipping in meetings with the Councils Enforcement Team, identifying speeding hotspots and engaging with officers on issues such as crime, anti-social behaviour and drugs.

Building a better Havering will only happen if we all work together. We should hold each other to high social standards - yet we should be wise to those who seek to exploit problems for their own gain rather than help solve them.

Across our Borough we need to make sure we have the transport infrastructure, public services and affordable housing in place that residents and families deserve. We also need a relentless focus on restoring Romford Town Centre to its former glory. The years of Conservative austerity - with pitiful investment and eye-watering cuts to council budgets, voted for by Rosindell - continue to hold us back.

Nationally I’ve stood up for the heartbeat of our communities – small businesses and community groups - who will play a critical role in regenerating our local economies. I have also led campaigns to support the most vulnerable children.

I'm now fighting locally to get Havering fit for the future - and to deliver that safer, cleaner, prouder Rush Green and Crowlands.

Angelina is a local parent and local authority worker who is committed to her community. She joined the Green Party because they share her values and determination to make life fairer for all of us who’ve had enough of the status quo.

Beth is a secondary school teacher and has worked with young people for over 15 years. Beth joined the Green Party because she knows politics can be better and she wants the council to do more to support people with the cost of living crisis.

Army Veteran, Community Policing volunteer and Father, I was raised in South Hornchurch and attended both Whybridge and Brittons schools. I have a stake in our future and I will not let our area continually decline, not just for my children, but for yours too. 📈

- Fight HMOs and Over-development burdening our critical infrastructure. 🏡
- Protect our Veterans, ensuring no veteran is forced to sleep rough.💂‍♂️
- Fight for community spaces, like our library, to end the Anti-Social Behaviour epidemic. 👥
- Clean up our service roads and stop them being abused by flytipping, drug crime and car theft. 🚨
- Cut Wasteful Spending. 💰

As current Chairman of your local Safer Neighbourhoods Ward Panel, I want a safer Havering that puts its residents first — ALWAYS.

Vote Reform on 7th May 🗳️

Ruth is an active campaigner for social, environment and disability justice in Havering.

She is a mother of two young girls, one of whom lives with profound and multiple learning disabilities.

Prior to her treasured role as a carer, she was an A Level lecturer at a sixth form college in Havering.

Since then, she's become an avid campaigner, writer and local theatre producer for disability inclusion.

For years, she's regularly held Havering Council to account on:
-The Launders Lane fires
-She led the campaign with Clear the Air in Havering that took Havering Council to the -High Court and won!
-She successfully lobbied Havering Council to support Zane’s Law.
-She is working with neuroscienctists and with Queen Mary's University towards -statutory long-term health accountability for Rainham residents.
-The importance of keeping Havering libraries open
-SEND rights for disabled children in the borough
-Environmental and health justice for children

Ruth has worked so hard outside of local government; it stands to reason how much more impact she could have as a force for good in the area if she was elected to represent South Hornchurch residents as your ward councillor.

A vote for Ruth is a vote for children's rights to better education, housing and health services; for anti-racism and for disability justice.

As a resident of south Havering, I don’t just live here, I’m invested in our future. My experience as a caseworker for our local MP has put me on the front lines, solving problems for residents one-on-one. This has given me the determination to ensure South Hornchurch is no longer overlooked.

On May 7th, you have two votes. I and Karen Bernard are proud to stand as your Labour candidates to provide the dedicated leadership this community deserves.

Our Promises to You:
• CCTV to tackle crime and fly-tipping.
• Resurface our roads and footpaths.
• Hold housing providers to account.
• Fight for more services in the ward.
• Be a strong local voice for all residents.

Community comes first. We will make sure South Hornchurch is NOT the forgotten part of Havering.

🗳️ Eric Sheqi ✔️
🗳️ Karen Bernard ✔️

About Mushtaq Ahmad.
Profession: Outreach Manager  
Education: BA (Hons.) Economics & Development Studies (SOAS: London), Former President of SOAS Students' Union
Resident of South Hornchurch Ward.

Candidate will strive for...

1. Investment in roads, lamposts for the improvements of the ward’s infrastructure. Many local roads have potholes and in need of new ashphalting.  Reflecting the fact, there has been no investment in local infrastructure for the last few years.

2. Currently there is no community space in the ward.  One centre that exists is not serving the communitys' needs.  It is vitally important to have sufficient community space to serve the growing community.

3. Library is not just a learning space but where people come together from all walks of life for learning and social cohesion. We need our library back.

4. For a community to flourish and to create a culture of care for everyone and togetherness. I want to bring the residents, especially its’ senior citizens, who contributed much to the ward under one roof to revitalise community spirit.

5. Food Bank – I want to forge links with the local supermarkets to supply surplus of food items to be distributed for free for people in need in the Ward.

6. This ward has gone through enormous demographic changes in the last few years, many people have made their home here.  Our representation body needs to reflect this demographic change and embrace it for the good.

7. There have been reports of crime such as burglaries; this must stop by making law enforcement more effective.

8. Unnecessary restriction on car movements in school areas creating much inconvenience for many residents, including incurrence of fines.  We must review this to make and keep transportation normal for everyone.  Children safety around school areas needs to be reviewed in light to alternative effective measures such as speed limit and cameras in place to enforce it rather than complete ban on cars during certain hours.

9. In bu

Mark works for an educational provider as IT systems coordinator, and worked previously with apps and automation. He wants to ensure the council’s £m’s IT transformation delivers to time, delivers value, and improves services. He founded Gidea Park Wombles in May 2024.

Madhu works for our local hospital and sees the pressure our health services are under. He wants to improve health outcomes for all ages, a proactive approach to tackling drug harms and champion better infrastructure planning.

Richard has worked for several international banks in the areas of operations, compliance risk management, and currently manages a team in tackling global financial crime.
Richard brings an analytical eye and a wealth of knowledge that could prove invaluable to the council.

Thomas Clarke lives with young family in Romford Constituency, in the London Borough of Havering, where he works as Ecommerce Content Director for Future Publishing. Thomas attended King Edward VI Grammar School in Chelmsford and went on to read Film and Television Studies at Brunel University in West London.

Thomas has been a volunteer in the local community since a teenager where he is now chairman of Harold Wood Cricket Club and the Essex Cricket Premier League. Thomas has a keen interest in the green spaces and wildlife in London understanding how vital a role these areas have on the local community.

Thomas became a member of the party following the EU Referendum of June 2016 – he is very keen to represent the area that he has live in for over 40 years.

Lawrence is an academic director of partnerships at an education provider, and has previously worked for the London Fire Brigade. Besides education, Lawrence also has a keen interest in planning law and development.

Arian is an economics researcher with financial and accountancy experience. Arian’s parents fled war and persecution in Afghanistan in 2001, moving from middle class surroundings, into extreme poverty and insecure housing in the UK. Arian grew up here with his elder brother, achieved citizenship, attended University, and has lived in Romford for the last 7 years.

Keira works in education with an interest in public heritage, especially in the power of local people improving their communities. As a mother to a toddler, Keira believes that everyone has the right to decent housing, a well-functioning NHS and a neighbourhood they can feel proud of.

Tia is a disability rights activist who has grown up and lived in St Andrews for her entire life. Tia has first hand experience of the difficulties that people are facing during the cost of living crisis, and has a great drive to speak up for under-represented residents of the borough.

Felix has lived in Havering with his family for 14 years. He works in software and is committed to creating a greener, fairer and more sustainable community. Felix believes in protecting local green spaces, improving public transport, and encouraging renewable energy initiatives.

Until I retired in 2018, I worked as a consultant in Obstetrics and Gynaecology at Queens Hospital.  During my long NHS career, I developed a range of transferable skills that I would like to use to make a positive contribution to the community. In addition to the obvious skills of listening to people and helping them to understand the pros and cons of difficult decisions, I was involved in the recruitment and training of resident doctors and the development of guidelines and protocols.
I am passionate about environmental issues, equity and fairness. I care about sustainability and believe that growing our own food is something we can do both as an enjoyable hobby and to help in the fight against climate change. I am fortunate to have an allotment in Havering and firmly believe that access to growing space should be available for more people. As a retired health professional, I am aware of the need for action to be taken to improve population health. I would like to see active transport such as walking, cycling and wheeling become safer and more desirable for the residents of Havering. 
My favourite place in the ward is the Lumiere Cinema a fantastic independent cinema where there is always something worthwhile on. I have always loved independent cinema and was pleased to be able to contribute to the Crowdfunding to get them started.  https://www.lumiereromford.com

I have lived in Havering for over 35 years. My three children were born here and educated in our local schools. Like many parents, I have watched them struggle to find the careers and opportunities they deserve. I didn’t choose a career in politics, but I can no longer stand by while successive governments failures make it harder for the next generation to build a life here.
My priority is building community wealth inspired by the successful Preston model, I will fight to ensure Havering Council awards contracts to local businesses first, keeping our money within our borough to create high quality jobs.
By combining my technical skills with a commitment to the environment, I will ensure every council decision is efficient, sustainable and focused on the long-term future of our children. It’s time for real change, led by someone who actually lives here and understands how to fix things.

Kieron is born, raised and lives in Romford. After commuting to University in London he began working in the Events and Entertainment industries. He believes in the equality and equity of all and would like to see St Edwards, Romford and Havering pioneer new legislation and systems for long term stability. In an ever more turbulent world he would like to help all residents to not just survive, but thrive.

Upminster has been my home for practically all of my life and it's a great feeling to stand in this election, hoping to represent my local area on Havering council.

I'm a sports enthusiast who enjoys watching football as well as playing badminton and running. My running has been a significant part of my life over the past year and I ran the London Marathon in April, raising over £3000 for a local charity.

Oh, I'm also level A2 certified in Spanish too because I always wanted to learn.

I've been involved in politics since 2021 for the Conservative Party, delivering countless leaflets across Havering and holding many conversations with residents. We know our country is stuck in a rut with high levels of taxation, high levels of immigration and services that aren't functioning in the way they should be.

However, nobody in this election can solve them. This election is about Upminster.

You may have heard about the poor state of council finances but the focus should be on what we can do. We live in a lovely area and that's our standard to maintain for years to come.

My key priorities are:

1. Fixing dangerous pavements - Repaving Argyle Gardens and Bridge Avenue
2. Enhancing Upminster Park facilities - Working with Friends of Upminster Park to offer more outdoor facilities near to the playing area such as a Padel court
3. High street deep cleaning - Cleaning Station Lane and Corbets Tey Road

Noel has lived in the Upminster area for over 40 years, a former owner of the towns original gelato parlour, Caffe Gelato, from 2015-20. A well known member of the business community for those years he is now out & about with Waitrose, keeping branches standards high. A family man with 3 grown up children, it’s organic gardening that keeps him busy now. With several elderly relatives to shop & watch out for life is never quiet.
